title: Splashdown! Apollo 15's Watery Homecoming
story: Splash! The Apollo 15 spacecraft plunged into the Pacific Ocean on August 7, 1971, ending its epic journey to the Moon. For nearly two weeks, astronauts had zoomed through space, driven a lunar rover, and collected Moon rocks. Now, bobbing in the waves, the crew waited for rescue ships to pluck them from the sea. bonus_fact: Apollo 15 was the first mission to use a lunar roving vehicle on the Moon's surface.

story: Imagine a radio so small it fits in your pocket! On August 7, 1955, Tokyo Telecommunications Engineering (later renamed Sony) launched its first transistor radios in Japan. title: Radio's Unsung Hero: Louis Alan Hazeltine
story: Imagine a world without radio. That's how it was before Louis Alan Hazeltine came along! Born on August 7, 1886, in Morristown, New Jersey, Hazeltine became an inventor who changed how we listen to music and news. His big breakthrough? The neutrodyne circuit. title: Mapping Australia's Coastline
story: Explorer Louis de Freycinet was born on August 7, 1779, in France. He became famous for creating the first complete map of Australia's coastline.
